name: United Arab Emirates

colors.symbolism: courage, valour, hardiness, and the unity of the seven emirates; common to the historical flags of the Trucial States
the fertility and abundance of the land
the defeat of enemies and the strength of mind in defending the nation; also the country's oil wealth

description: A red vertical band at the hoist taking up one-quarter of the flag's length, alongside three equal horizontal stripes filling the remaining three-quarters: green at the top, white in the middle, black at the bottom.
history: Adopted on 2 December 1971 with the formation of the United Arab Emirates from the previously British-protected Trucial States. Designed by Abdullah Mohammed Al Maainah, then a 19-year-old student, whose entry was selected from over a thousand submissions in a national competition. The colours are the four Pan-Arab colours of the Arab Revolt of 1916, used here to express the country's Arab identity and unity.

features.symbolism: the four Pan-Arab colours (red, green, white, black) of the Arab Revolt; a vertical red band at the hoist with three equal horizontal stripes — green, white, black — filling the rest of the field
